title: Fresh Dense Kenyan Cauliflower Curry
description: A kid-friendly, kosher-friendly Kenyan dish featuring tender cauliflower in a rich and creamy curry sauce, made with fresh ingredients and cooked to perfection in a tandoor-inspired style.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C) to mimic the tandoor cooking style.
In a large bowl, toss the cauliflower florets with 1 tablespoon of coconut oil, salt, and black pepper until they are evenly coated.
Spread the cauliflower on a baking sheet and roast in the oven for 20-25 minutes, or until tender and lightly browned.
In a large pan, heat the remaining 1 tablespoon of coconut oil over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to brown, about 5-7 minutes.
Add the grated ginger and minced garlic and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly.
Stir in the curry powder and garam masala and cook for 1 minute, until the spices are fragrant.
Pour in the canned coconut milk and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Add the roasted cauliflower to the curry sauce and stir to coat.
Reduce the heat to low and let the curry simmer for 10-15 minutes, or until the sauce has thickened and the flavors have melded together.
